title: Excluding users or groups activities from audit logs is security-sensitive
description: Audit logs are critical for detecting suspicious activity, investigating incidents, and meeting compliance requirements. Excluding specific users or groups from audit logging creates blind spots, allowing malicious or accidental actions to go undetected. All user and group activities should be logged unless there is a strong, documented justification.
message: Excluding users or groups from audit logs creates security blind spots. Ensure all activities are logged.
impact: Unlogged activities can hide malicious actions, hinder incident response, and result in compliance violations.
recommendation: Configure audit logging to include all users and groups. Only exclude accounts with a documented, risk-assessed justification, and review exclusions regularly.

noncompliant.description: Excludes all actions by the admin group from audit logs, creating a security blind spot.

compliant.description: All user and group activities are logged, supporting full auditability.

why_is_this_an_issue: Excluding users or groups from audit logs can allow malicious or accidental actions to go undetected, undermining security and compliance.
how_to_fix: Review audit log configurations. Remove any exclusions for users or groups unless strictly necessary and justified. Monitor for unauthorized changes to logging policies.
